title: Before Knowledge
content:
When I walked roseless tracks and wide,
    Ere dawned your date for meeting me,
    O why did you not cry Halloo
    Across the stretch between, and say:

    "We move, while years as yet divide,
    On closing lines which - though it be
    You know me not nor I know you -
    Will intersect and join some day!"

     Then well I had borne
     Each scraping thorn;
     But the winters froze,
     And grew no rose;
     No bridge bestrode
     The gap at all;
     No shape you showed,
     And I heard no call!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Thomas Hardy delivers a powerful performance in "Before Knowledge"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
